info.title: IoT DC3 REST API
info.description:
IoT DC3 platform REST API documentation.

Covers all service modules: auth (authentication and authorization),
manager (device/driver/point/profile configuration), data (telemetry,
events, commands, notifications), and agentic (AI chat and tools).

Authentication: obtain a salt and token via /api/v3/auth/token/*, then
use the Authorize button to set X-Auth-Tenant, X-Auth-Login, and
X-Auth-Token. X-Auth-Token is a JSON object {"salt":"...","token":"..."}.

license.name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

tags.name: dashboard
tags.description: Data monitoring dashboard configuration: manage data-side dashboard layouts, widgets, and visualization preferences for device data monitoring

tags.name: rule
tags.description: Data processing rule definitions: manage filtering, transformation, and routing pipelines that process device data streams through the rule engine

tags.name: notify_history
tags.description: Notification delivery history: query alert dispatch records including timestamps, target channels, and delivery status for audit and troubleshooting

tags.name: command_history
tags.description: Command execution audit trail: query historical records of commands sent to devices including execution status, timestamps, and response data

tags.name: driver_status
tags.description: Driver operational status: query current connected, disconnected, and error states of protocol driver instances

tags.name: point_command_history
tags.description: Point command audit trail: query historical records of commands dispatched to device data points including timestamps and execution outcomes

tags.name: point_value
tags.description: Data point values: query real-time snapshots and historical time-series values collected from industrial device data points

tags.name: event_history
tags.description: Device event audit trail: query historical records of device alarms, state changes, and status transitions with timestamps and event payloads

tags.name: rule_state
tags.description: Rule execution states: manage rule engine states including activation, suspension, and runtime statistics for data processing rules

tags.name: message
tags.description: Platform messages: query messages exchanged between services and devices over the message bus including data reports, commands, and system notifications

tags.name: notify_channel_bind
tags.description: Notification-to-channel bindings: route specific notification rules to delivery channels for targeted alert distribution

tags.name: notify_channel
tags.description: Notification channel definitions: manage delivery mechanisms for alerts including email, SMS, webhook, and message-bus channels with endpoint configuration

tags.name: notify
tags.description: Notification rule definitions: manage alert rules that trigger on device events, data thresholds, and status changes with configurable severity and routing

tags.name: device_status
tags.description: Device operational status: query current online, offline, fault, and maintenance states of registered industrial devices

tags.name: point_command
tags.description: Point-level command dispatch: send commands to individual device data points and track their execution status in real time

post.description: Update the trigger conditions or actions of a rule owned by the current tenant. Tenant ownership is verified before the change is applied.
Page through the runtime states of rules (enabled/disabled, last-fired, counters) for the current tenant. Use to survey which rules are active and how often each has fired.
Page through alarm and automation rules owned by the current tenant, filtered by the query body. Use to browse or locate rules for inspection; results are scoped to the caller's tenant.
Delete a rule owned by the current tenant by its ID. Tenant ownership is verified before deletion; use to retire a rule that should no longer fire.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create an alarm or automation rule (trigger conditions plus actions) for the current tenant. Use to register a new rule the engine evaluates against point values or events.
Page through stored time-series readings for points under a device (tenant-scoped). Use to query raw historical values; results are ordered by collection time.
Return the most recent reading for each point under a device for the current tenant, paged by the request query. Use to read near-real-time snapshots; results are ordered by collection time.

Page through the point command audit trail for the current tenant, filterable by device, point, command type and status. Use to review which commands were dispatched to data points and whether each succeeded.

Dispatch a downward write command to push a new value onto a single point on the tenant's device and return its command ID. Poll the command history with the returned ID to confirm whether the write reached the device; the optional commandId makes submission idempotent.

Dispatch a downward read command to fetch the current value of a single point on the tenant's device and return its command ID. Poll the command history with the returned ID to track execution status; the optional commandId makes submission idempotent.
Update an existing notification rule (routing, severity, enable flag) for the current tenant. Ownership is validated before the update is applied.
Page through notification (alert-routing) rules for the current tenant with optional query filters. Use to enumerate which rules govern alert dispatch.
Page through notifications already dispatched for the current tenant, filterable by channel and delivery status. Use to audit what was sent and whether each channel succeeded.
Delete a notification rule by ID for the current tenant. Ownership is validated before deletion, so cross-tenant records cannot be removed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Update an existing notification channel's endpoint and configuration for the current tenant. Use to rotate webhook URLs, credentials, or recipient lists; the owning tenant is verified before mutation.
Page through notification delivery channels owned by the current tenant. Use to enumerate available channels for rule binding; results are filtered by the query and returned as a paginated page.
Delete a notification channel by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Use to retire a delivery channel whose endpoint is no longer valid; existing NotifyHistory records are preserved.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Update an existing notification-to-channel binding (rule, channel, enable flag) for the current tenant. Ownership is validated before the change is applied.
Page through notification-to-channel bindings for the current tenant, filterable by notifyId, channelId and enable flag. Use to discover which delivery channels a rule fires through.
Delete a notification-to-channel binding by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Use to stop routing a rule's alerts through a channel; ownership is validated before deletion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Bind a delivery channel to a notification rule for the current tenant, so alerts from that rule route through the channel. Returns add-success status.

Create a notification delivery channel (email, SMS, webhook, or message bus) for the current tenant. Returns add-success status; the channel must be bound to a rule via NotifyChannelBind before it can dispatch.
Create a notification (alert-routing) rule for the current tenant that wires alarm triggers to delivery channels. Use to define how and where alerts are dispatched.
Update the name, code, level or enabled flag of an existing alarm message template owned by the current tenant. The tenant scope is enforced from the authenticated caller, not the request body.
Page through the current tenant's alarm message templates, filterable by name, code, level and enabled flag. Use to browse or locate templates for reuse by alarm rules and notification channels.
Delete a tenant-owned alarm message template by its ID. Returns not-found if the record belongs to another tenant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a new alarm message template scoped to the current tenant. Use to register a reusable message (name, code, level, enabled flag) that alarm rules and notifications can reference.

Record a device event (alarm, state change, or status transition) reported by a device for the current tenant and return the new record ID. Use when a device reports an event that must be appended to the audit trail.

Page through device event records (alarms, state changes, status transitions) for the current tenant, filtered by the query body. Use to browse the append-only event audit trail; results are ordered by event time.
Return the current ONLINE/OFFLINE status of each driver instance for the current tenant, keyed by driver id. Page through driver and pagination filters; results are tenant-scoped.

Return the current status (ONLINE, OFFLINE, MAINTAIN, FAULT) of the current tenant's devices as a device-id-to-status map. Use to read live connectivity state; pass a DeviceQuery to filter by driver, profile or other dimensions.
Remove the acknowledgement flag from a single alert (by source and record id) for the current tenant. Returns true when the row was actually updated.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Alert source scope; one of driver, device, or point

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the alert record within the given source; must belong to the current tenant

post.description: Page through the tenant's alert records with filters for source (driver/device/point), alarm type, confirm flag and a time window. Use to browse or triage the alert history table.
Mark a single alert (by source and record id) as acknowledged for the current tenant. Returns true when the row was actually updated.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Alert source scope; one of driver, device, or point

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the alert record within the given source; must belong to the current tenant

post.description: Apply confirm or unconfirm to a list of alerts (each {source, id}) for the current tenant in one call. Returns the count of rows actually changed.

Page through command-call history for the current tenant with filters such as device, command and execution status over a time window. Returns a page of records; use to audit which commands ran and their results.
Send a downward control command to a device for the current tenant and record the call in command history. Returns the new history record ID; use it to poll the execution result and response data.

get.description: Return the runtime state of one rule (enabled/disabled, last-fired, counters) for the current tenant. Use to inspect a single rule's execution status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return one rule's full definition (conditions, actions, owner) for the current tenant. Use to inspect or edit a specific rule before updating or deleting it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Point Value History by Device and Point
get.description: Return the most recent time-series values for one point on one device for the current tenant, as a list of raw value strings bounded by count (default 100). Use to read a single point's latest history.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device; must belong to the current tenant

parameters.name: point_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the point whose history is being queried; must belong to a profile attached to the device

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Maximum number of historical values to return; defaults to 100 when omitted

get.summary: Get Point Command History by Command ID
get.description: Return the full audit record for a single point command identified by its command ID (tenant-scoped). Use to look up the request value, response value, status, error and timing of one dispatched command.

parameters.name: commandId
parameters.description: Identifier of the point command to look up; must reference a command dispatched under the current tenant.

get.description: Return a single dispatched-notification record by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect one notification's timestamp, target channel, and delivery status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return a single notification rule for the current tenant. Use to inspect routing, severity and channel bindings for one rule.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return a single notification channel with its endpoint configuration, scoped to the current tenant. Use to inspect how a rule's alerts will be delivered before binding or firing.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Notification Channel Binding by ID
get.description: Return the full detail of a single notification-to-channel binding by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Returns the rule, channel, enable flag and extension attributes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return one alarm message template (name, code, level, enabled flag) by its ID, restricted to the current tenant. Use to inspect a single template before updating or deleting it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Event History by Record ID
get.description: Fetch a single device event record by its record ID, tenant-scoped. Returns the event type, payload, and timestamp; use to inspect one specific reported event.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Identifier of the event history record to fetch; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Count devices currently online under one driver for the current tenant. Pass the driver id; returns a single count.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose online device count is queried; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Count devices currently offline under one driver for the current tenant. Pass the driver id; returns a single count.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose offline device count is queried; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the current status (ONLINE, OFFLINE, MAINTAIN, FAULT) of every device the current tenant associates with the given profile. Use to check connectivity of all devices sharing a profile template; results are a device-id-to-status map.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the profile template; must belong to the current tenant. Only devices bound to this profile are returned.

get.description: Return the current status (ONLINE, OFFLINE, MAINTAIN, FAULT) of every device the current tenant runs under the given driver. Use to inspect connectivity across the whole fleet a single driver manages; results are a device-id-to-status map.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver; must belong to the current tenant. Only devices managed by this driver are returned.

get.description: Rank the tenant's devices, points or drivers by activity over a rolling window (range_key or range_hours), returning the top N entries for the chosen dimension. Use to surface the busiest entities.

parameters.name: dimension
parameters.description: Ranking dimension defining what kind of entity to rank; one of device, point, or driver

parameters.name: range_hours
parameters.description: Rolling time window length in hours; used only when range_key is omitted

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding range_hours; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of ranked entries to return

get.description: Return a platform-wide health snapshot: center and infra probe status plus tenant-scoped driver and device fleet summaries. Use to populate the home banner health widget.
Return the N most recent point-value readings across the tenant's points, newest first. Use to populate the live feed panel on a user-triggered refresh.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent readings to return, newest first

get.description: Return today's, yesterday's and cumulative counts (devices, points, alerts) with day-over-day change ratios for the current tenant. Powers the dashboard headline tiles.

Bucket today's, yesterday's and cumulative device/point/alert counts into hour or day buckets over a rolling window (range_key or range_hours) for the current tenant. Use to render the dashboard trend chart.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Time bucket granularity for the trend chart; hour groups by hour-of-day, day groups by calendar date

parameters.name: range_hours
parameters.description: Rolling time window length in hours; used only when range_key is omitted

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding range_hours; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.description: Bucket the tenant's point-value collection latencies over a rolling window (range_key or range_hours) into histogram bands. Use to assess how fresh the ingested readings are.

parameters.name: range_hours
parameters.description: Rolling time window length in hours; used only when range_key is omitted

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding range_hours; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.description: Aggregate the tenant's data-collection activity into hour-of-day cells over a rolling window (default 168 hours / one week). Use to render the activity heatmap and spot quiet or peak hours.

parameters.name: range_hours
parameters.description: Rolling time window length in hours; default 168 (one week). Used only when range_key is omitted

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding range_hours; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.description: Detect the tenant's points or devices that have produced no new data beyond a silence threshold, compared against a baseline day range. Use to catch sensors or devices that have stopped reporting.

parameters.name: baseline_days
parameters.description: Baseline rolling day range used to learn each entity's expected reporting cadence

parameters.name: silent_minutes
parameters.description: Silence threshold in minutes; an entity with no reading for this long is flagged silent

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of silent sources to return

get.description: Return per-protocol health and connectivity status for the tenant's driver fleet. Use to see which protocols are online, degraded, or offline.
Summarize the tenant's data-coverage gaps, listing points or devices missing expected readings. Use to find blind spots in collection coverage.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of points or devices missing expected readings to include in the gap summary

get.description: Bucket the tenant's alerts by alarm type over a rolling day range. Use to see which alarm categories dominate within the window.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range over which alerts are bucketed by alarm type

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding days; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.description: Return daily alert counts over a rolling day range for the current tenant. Use to visualize whether alert volume is rising or falling; each item is one day's count.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range for the trend; one point per day is returned

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding days; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.description: Rank the tenant's alert sources (driver/device/point) by alert volume over a rolling day range, returning the top N. Use to find which entities generate the most alerts.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range over which alert volume is counted

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding days; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of top sources to return

get.description: Detect the tenant's alert storms by flagging sources whose alert count inside a short rolling hour window exceeds a threshold. Use to spot bursty or flooding alert producers.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Rolling detection window length in hours

parameters.name: min_count
parameters.description: Minimum alert count within the window for a source to be flagged as a storm

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of storm sources to return

get.description: Return the tenant's current alert totals: total count, unconfirmed count and a breakdown by alarm type. Use to populate the alert summary cards.
Compare each peer entity's alert rate against the cohort baseline over a rolling day range and flag statistically significant deviations. Use to catch entities that alert far more than their peers.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range used to build the cohort baseline and measure each peer's alert rate

get.description: Return the mean time to acknowledge alerts per day over a rolling day range for the current tenant. Use to track whether the team is getting faster at acknowledging alerts.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range for the MTTA trend; one mean-time value per day is returned

get.description: Return the N most recent alerts for the current tenant, newest first. Use to populate the recent-alerts list; each item carries source, alarm type, message and confirm state.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent alerts to return, newest first

get.description: Flag the tenant's alert sources that toggle confirm and recovery state repeatedly inside a rolling hour window above a count threshold. Use to find noisy, oscillating alerts that may need suppression.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Rolling detection window length in hours

parameters.name: min_count
parameters.description: Minimum confirm/recovery toggle count within the window for a source to be flagged as flapping

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of flapping sources to return

get.description: Return alert source pairs that fire within a tight time window of each other over a rolling hour range, scored by co-occurrence. Use to discover alerts that share an upstream root cause.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Rolling detection window length in hours

parameters.name: window_sec
parameters.description: Maximum gap in seconds between two alerts to count them as co-occurring

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of correlated source pairs to return

get.description: Correlate the tenant's configuration changes with subsequent alert-volume shifts over a rolling day range, returning the top-impacted items. Use to confirm whether a config edit improved or hurt alert noise.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range over which config changes are correlated with alert-volume shifts

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of impacted items to return

get.description: Bucket the tenant's currently unconfirmed alerts by elapsed age for the current tenant. Use to surface stale alerts that are still awaiting acknowledgement.
Aggregate the tenant's alerts into hour-of-day-by-day cells over a rolling day range. Use to render the alert heatmap and surface peak alerting hours.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling day range spanning the heatmap's day axis

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time range key overriding days; one of today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

get.summary: Get Command History by Record ID
get.description: Fetch one command-call history record by its record ID (tenant-scoped), including execution status, timestamps and the device response. Use to check the outcome of a command issued via Call Command.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Record ID returned by the Call Command endpoint; must resolve to a command-call history entry owned by the current tenant

RString.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

Condition.description: Deterministic rule condition describing the field, operator and comparison values.

field.description: Source field to evaluate, for example numValue, status, or age.

operator.description: Operator code, for example >, >=, <, <=, ==, !=, between, outside, silence.

expected.description: Expected string value for status-like rules.

Content.description: Rule extended content, holding the condition, window, recovery and metadata of a rule.

window.description: Evaluation window, expressed with ISO-8601 durations where time is needed.

eventType.description: Event type produced when the rule is matched.

labels.description: Business labels for filtering and dashboards.

Recovery.description: Optional recovery condition describing when an alarm is considered recovered.

duration.description: ISO-8601 duration that must remain recovered.

RuleExt.description: JSON extension object for rule configuration, carrying extended information related to rules.

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content, which can be distinguished by type and version.

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

alarmTargetTypeFlag.description: Alarm target type: POINT, DEVICE, DRIVER, or EVENT. Specifies which entity type this rule monitors.

ruleName.description: Rule name. Unique name within a tenant for identifying a data processing pipeline rule.

ruleCode.description: Rule code. Stable business identifier; must not change once deployed.

entityId.description: ID of the entity (point, device, driver, or event) that this rule monitors, as selected by alarmTargetTypeFlag.

notifyId.description: ID of the notification rule to invoke when this rule triggers.

messageId.description: ID of the message template to use for the alert payload of this rule.

ruleExt.description: Rule extension information, serialized as JSON for custom conditions and actions.

Window.description: Evaluation window defining the aggregation mode, duration and minimum sample count.

mode.description: Evaluation mode, for example LAST, ALL, ANY, AVG, MIN, MAX, SUM, COUNT.

minSamples.description: Minimum samples required in the window.

current.description: Current page number, starting from 1; ignored when count is disabled

size.description: Number of records per page; clamped to the configured page-size limit

startTime.description: Inclusive start timestamp (epoch milliseconds) for time-range filtering

endTime.description: Inclusive end timestamp (epoch milliseconds) for time-range filtering

orders.description: Sort order items applied to the query results

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation. Required for query scope.

RPageRuleStateVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

RuleStateExt.description: JSON extension object for rule runtime state metadata, embedded inside VO extension fields

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content holding the rule runtime state snapshot

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

ruleId.description: ID of the rule whose execution state is recorded.

firstTriggerTime.description: Timestamp when the rule first triggered after activation.

lastTriggerTime.description: Timestamp of the most recent rule trigger.

lastRecoverTime.description: Timestamp when the rule last transitioned from triggered to recovered state.

lastNotifyTime.description: Timestamp when the last notification was dispatched for this rule.

triggerCount.description: Cumulative count of times this rule has triggered since activation.

alarmId.description: ID of the active alarm associated with this rule state.

entityStateExt.description: Entity state extension information, serialized as JSON for custom runtime metadata.

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation. Required for query scope.

ruleName.description: Filter by rule name. Supports partial matching.

ruleCode.description: Filter by rule code. Exact match on the stable business identifier.

RPageRuleVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation. Required for query scope.

deviceId.description: Filter by device ID. Returns results scoped to a specific device.

deviceName.description: Filter by device name. Supports partial matching.

pointName.description: Filter by data point name. Supports partial matching.

createTimeFrom.description: Start time for querying point values

rangeHours.description: Fallback rolling time range in hours

rangeKey.description: Preset time range key: today, 24h, 7d, or 30d

deviceId.description: ID of the device that produced this value.

pointId.description: ID of the data point this value belongs to.

rawValue.description: Raw value as received directly from the device protocol.

calValue.description: Calculated (engineering) value after applying base value, multiplier, and decimal formatting.

numValue.description: Numeric representation of the calculated value for sorting and aggregation.

hasLatestValue.description: Whether the latest-value query returned a real sampled value (true) or a fallback placeholder (false).

driverId.description: ID of the driver that collected this value.

tenantId.description: Identifier of the tenant that owns this point value.

createTime.description: Timestamp (ISO-8601) when this point value was first created.

operateTime.description: Timestamp (ISO-8601) of the most recent update or refresh of this point value.

RPagePointValueVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

deviceId.description: Identifier of the device to filter by; must belong to the current tenant.

pointId.description: Identifier of the data point to filter by; must belong to the current tenant.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the command to filter by (e.g. pending, success, failed).

type.description: Type of the point command to filter by (e.g. read, write).

page.description: Current page number for pagination, starting at 1.

commandId.description: ID of the command definition invoked.

deviceId.description: ID of the device the point command was sent to.

pointId.description: ID of the target data point.

requestValue.description: Command value sent to the data point.

responseValue.description: Response value received from the data point after command execution.

errorCode.description: Error code if the point command execution failed. Null on success.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error message if the point command execution failed. Null on success.

sourceUserId.description: ID of the user who issued this point command.

occurTime.description: Timestamp when the point command was issued.

sendTime.description: Timestamp when the point command was dispatched.

finishTime.description: Timestamp when the point command execution completed.

expireTime.description: Timestamp when the point command expires without a response.

RPagePointCommandHistoryVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

deviceId.description: ID of the device to write the point value to.

pointId.description: ID of the data point to write to.

value.description: Value to write to the data point. Must conform to the point type and any validation constraints.

commandId.description: ID of the write command to invoke.

deviceId.description: ID of the device to read the point value from.

pointId.description: ID of the data point to read.

commandId.description: ID of the read command to invoke.

NotifyExt.description: JSON extension object for notification configuration, embedded inside VO extension fields

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended notification content, distinguished by the type and version fields of the base extension

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

notifyName.description: Notification template name; must be unique within the current tenant.

notifyCode.description: Stable business code identifying this notification template; should not change once deployed.

autoConfirmFlag.description: Whether alarms raised by this template are auto-confirmed (AUTO) or require manual confirmation (MANUAL).

notifyInterval.description: Minimum cooldown interval, in seconds, between successive notification deliveries to prevent alert storms.

notifyExt.description: Notification extension information, serialized as JSON for custom delivery logic (channels, templates, recipients).

enableFlag.description: Whether this notification template is active: ENABLE (0) to enable or DISABLE (1) to disable.

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation. Required for query scope.

notifyName.description: Filter by notification name. Supports partial matching.

notifyCode.description: Filter by notification code. Exact match on the stable business identifier.

notifyInterval.description: Filter by notification cooldown interval in milliseconds. Exact match.

RPageNotifyVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

notifyId.description: Filter by notification definition ID. Exact match.

messageId.description: Filter by message template ID used for the notification. Exact match.

channelId.description: Filter by delivery channel ID. Exact match.

alarmId.description: Filter by associated alarm ID. Exact match.

channelTypeFlag.description: Filter by notification channel type (e.g. EMAIL, SMS, WEBHOOK). Exact match.

statusFlag.description: Filter by notification delivery status. Exact match.

ruleId.description: ID of the rule that triggered this notification.

notifyId.description: ID of the notification definition used.

messageId.description: ID of the message template used for this notification.

channelId.description: ID of the notification channel through which this notification was delivered.

alarmId.description: ID of the alarm associated with this notification.

channelTypeFlag.description: Channel type used to deliver this notification (e.g. EMAIL, SMS, WEBHOOK).

target.description: Notification target address or identifier (e.g. email address, phone number, webhook URL).

statusFlag.description: Delivery status of this notification record (e.g. SUCCESS, FAILED, PENDING).

requestExt.description: Request payload sent to the notification channel, serialized as JSON.

responseExt.description: Response received from the notification channel, serialized as JSON.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the notification delivery failed. Null on success.

retryCount.description: Number of delivery retries attempted for this notification.

RPageMessageVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

eventCode.description: Stable business code of the event; must match a defined event code.

paramValues.description: Parameter values submitted with this event report, keyed by parameter code.

message.description: Free-text detail or payload accompanying the event report.

deviceId.description: Identifier of the device to filter by; must belong to the current tenant.

eventId.description: Identifier of the event definition to filter by; must belong to the current tenant.

eventCode.description: Code of the event to filter by.

eventTypeFlag.description: Type of the event to filter by.

page.description: Pagination parameters: page number and page size.

recordId.description: Unique record ID for this event history entry.

deviceId.description: ID of the device that triggered this event.

eventCode.description: Event code. Stable business identifier for this event.

message.example: High temperature alarm triggered: 85°C exceeds 80°C threshold

occurTime.description: Timestamp when the event occurred on the device.

receiveTime.description: Timestamp when the platform received the event report.

acknowledgeTime.description: Timestamp when a user acknowledged this event. Null if not yet acknowledged.

acknowledgeUserId.description: ID of the user who acknowledged this event. Null if not yet acknowledged.

AlertBulkConfirmVO.description: Bulk confirm/unconfirm request for the dashboard alert panel

confirm.description: Direction of the bulk operation: true sets confirm_flag to 1 (confirmed), false resets it to 0 (unconfirmed). Defaults to true when null.

items.description: Alert targets to apply the bulk operation to; each entry identifies a single alert row in the current tenant scope.

Item.description: Single alert target for the bulk operation

source.description: Alert source classification: "device" for device-originated alerts or "driver" for driver-originated alerts; must match a value stored in dc3_entity_alert.source.

id.description: Primary key of the alert row in dc3_entity_alert to confirm or unconfirm.

RInteger.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

deviceId.description: Filter by device ID; the device must belong to the current tenant.

commandId.description: Filter by command definition ID; the command must belong to the current tenant.

commandCode.description: Filter by exact command code, e.g. a driver-defined read/write opcode.

status.description: Filter by command lifecycle status (PENDING, SENT, SUCCESS, FAILED, TIMEOUT, EXPIRED, DEAD, DUPLICATE).

recordId.description: Unique record ID for this command history entry.

deviceId.description: ID of the device the command was sent to.

commandId.description: ID of the command definition invoked.

commandCode.description: Command code. Stable business identifier for this command.

resultValues.description: Result values returned from the device after command execution, keyed by parameter code.

errorCode.description: Error code if the command execution failed. Null on success.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error message if the command execution failed. Null on success.

sourceUserId.description: ID of the user who issued this command.

occurTime.description: Timestamp when the command was issued.

sendTime.description: Timestamp when the command was dispatched to the device.

finishTime.description: Timestamp when the command execution completed (success or failure).

expireTime.description: Timestamp when the command expires. If the device does not respond by this time, the command is considered failed.

BucketVO.description: Alert count for a single type bucket

RAlertStatsVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

PeerDeviationVO.description: A device whose alarm rate deviates sharply from its profile peers

profileId.description: profile ID the device belongs to

peerMedian.description: median alarm count across the profile peers

ratio.description: alarmCount divided by peerMedian; 0 means peerMedian was 0

RListPeerDeviationVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

MttaTrendVO.description: One day's MTTA (mean time to acknowledge) statistic

date.description: ISO date of the data point

confirmedCount.description: count of confirmed events used in the percentile calculation for this day

RListMttaTrendVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

RListAlertItemVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

FlappingSourceVO.description: A source/event-type pair that fired repeatedly in the window (flapping)

count.description: number of times this source/type fired in the window

RListFlappingSourceVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

CorrelationPairVO.description: Two events that frequently co-occur, suggesting a cascading-failure relationship

coCount.description: number of times A and B co-occurred

RListCorrelationPairVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

ChangeImpactVO.description: One config-change event overlaid on the alarm trend chart

kind.description: changed entity kind: driver, device or profile

RListChangeImpactVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

under1h.description: count of unconfirmed alarms aged under 1 hour

h1to6.description: count of unconfirmed alarms aged 1 to 6 hours

h6to24.description: count of unconfirmed alarms aged 6 to 24 hours

over24h.description: count of unconfirmed alarms aged over 24 hours (SLA breach indicator)

total.description: convenience sum, equals the total unconfirmed count

RAgingBacklogVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

AlertActivityCellVO.description: Event-overview alarm heatmap cell (day-of-week by hour-of-day)

dow.description: Day of week, 0 to 6 matching Postgres EXTRACT(DOW) where 0 = Sunday

count.description: Number of alarms aggregated in this day-of-week/hour-of-day cell

RListAlertActivityCellVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.
